Test-Retest Reliability of Core Lexicon Analysis

Authors
Affiliations

Sarah Grace Dalton

University of Georgia, Department of Communication Sciences and Special Education

Robert Cavanaugh

MGH Institute of Health Professions, School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences

Published

February 26, 2026

Load packages and setup file reading

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(lme4)
library(readxl)
library(boot)
library(flextable)
library(easystats)
library(officer)

source("gtheory-icc.R")
set.seed(42)

raw_data <- read_csv(here::here("data", "corelex-deid-data.csv"))

bootstrap_n = 1000

Data Cleaning

all_data = raw_data |> 
  select(-difference) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("t"), 
               names_to = "time", 
               values_to = "score") |> 
  rename(stimuli = sheet_name) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor),
         participant = as.factor(paste0("p", str_extract(participant, "^[^.]*")))) |> 
  mutate(max_score = case_when(
    stimuli == "Cat" ~ 35,
    stimuli == "Cinderella" ~ 95,
    stimuli == "Sandwich" ~ 25,
    stimuli == "Umbrella" ~ 36,
    stimuli == "Window" ~ 24,
    TRUE ~ NA
  )) |> 
  mutate(percent_acc = score/max_score)

knitr::kable(head(all_data))
stimuli participant dx time score max_score percent_acc
Cat pP49 pwa t1 25 35 0.7142857
Cat pP49 pwa t2 30 35 0.8571429
Cat pP101 pwa t1 25 35 0.7142857
Cat pP101 pwa t2 24 35 0.6857143
Cat pP65 pwa t1 0 35 0.0000000
Cat pP65 pwa t2 5 35 0.1428571

Visualize test-retest

  • did not use thsi figure; see figure at end of doc with 95% PI
all_wide = all_data |> select(-score) |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = time, values_from = percent_acc) 

p1 = all_wide |> 
  mutate(dx = ifelse(dx == "control", "Healthy Control", "Individuals with Aphasia")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = t1, y = t2, fill = dx)) +
  #geom_point() + 
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.8, size = 2, shape = 21, color = "lightgrey", width = .015, height = 0.015) +
  geom_abline(alpha = 0.6) + 
  facet_wrap(~stimuli, ncol = 3) + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), labels = scales::label_percent(), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), labels = scales::label_percent(), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", aspect.ratio = 1) +
  labs(fill = "", x = "Percent of Checklist Produced, Time 1", y = "Percent of Checklist Produced, Time 2") +
  coord_fixed()

ggsave(plot = p1, here::here("output" , "figure 1.png"), height = 7, width = 9)

p1

Estimating reliability under a G-theory framework; Revision: Binomial Model

In our revision, we adjusted the model to take the proportion of core lex words produced rather than just the count. We need to check for overdispersion. This was run for each combination of the models. Dispersion ratios farther away from 1 (p<0.05) indicate over or underdispersion, the former being more problematic. In this case, the data are equidispersed.

overdispersion <- all_data |>
  group_nest(stimuli, dx) |>
  mutate(
    od = map(data, ~{
      m <- glmer(cbind(score, max_score - score) ~ 1 + (1|participant) + (1|time),
                 data = .x, family = binomial())
      performance::check_overdispersion(m)
    }),
    dispersion_ratio = map_dbl(od, ~.x$dispersion_ratio),
    p_value = map_dbl(od, ~.x$p_value)
  ) |>
  select(stimuli, dx, dispersion_ratio, p_value)

knitr::kable(overdispersion)
stimuli dx dispersion_ratio p_value
Cat control 1.1002694 0.496
Cat pwa 1.0119786 0.984
Cinderella control 1.0714470 0.728
Cinderella pwa 0.9848613 0.904
Sandwich control 1.0613141 0.640
Sandwich pwa 1.0137899 0.912
Umbrella control 1.0978026 0.464
Umbrella pwa 1.0030880 1.000
Window control 0.9782793 0.984
Window pwa 1.0244964 0.912 
# Run ICC analysis on all stimuli / dx
icc_A1 <- all_data |>
  group_nest(stimuli, dx) |>
  mutate(
    icc_results = map(data,
                      ~calc_icc_formula_binomial(cbind(score, max_score - score) ~ 1 + (1|participant) + (1|time),
                      "participant", .x, R = bootstrap_n))
  )

# Extract ICC results table
icc_table <- icc_A1 |>
  mutate(icc_data = map(icc_results, "icc_results")) |>
  select(stimuli, dx, icc_data) |>
  unnest(icc_data)

# Extract variance components table  
variance_table <- icc_A1 |>
  mutate(var_data = map(icc_results, "variance_components")) |>
  select(stimuli, dx, var_data) |>
  unnest(var_data)

manuscript_table = icc_table |> 
  select(Stimuli = stimuli, Group = dx, Dependability = icc, `95% CI` = `ci_95`) |> 
  flextable() |> 
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>   # Make sure table is left-aligned
  flextable::set_caption(apa_caption("Table X. Dependability Coefficients by Stimuli and Group"), align_with_table = TRUE,
                         word_stylename = "Normal",
                         fp_p = fp_par(text.align = "left")) |>
  flextable::autofit(add_w = 0, add_h = 1) |>
  flextable::add_footer_lines(values = "Note: Dependability coefficients are analogous to intraclass correlations for agreement") |>
  theme_apa2()

# To save it as a word document:
doc <- officer::read_docx()
# add the table to the document
doc <- body_add_flextable(doc, value = manuscript_table)
# filename within the path we want
print(doc, target = "output/icc_by_stimuli_and_group_revision.docx")


knitr::kable(icc_table)
stimuli dx icc ci_lower ci_upper ci_95 formula bootstrap_success_rate
Cat control 0.64 0.46 0.74 [0.46, 0.74] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Cat pwa 0.88 0.81 0.92 [0.81, 0.92] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Cinderella control 0.88 0.79 0.93 [0.79, 0.93] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Cinderella pwa 0.96 0.93 0.98 [0.93, 0.98] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Sandwich control 0.59 0.39 0.69 [0.39, 0.69] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Sandwich pwa 0.85 0.77 0.89 [0.77, 0.89] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Umbrella control 0.56 0.39 0.65 [0.39, 0.65] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Umbrella pwa 0.88 0.78 0.92 [0.78, 0.92] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Window control 0.49 0.35 0.57 [0.35, 0.57] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1
Window pwa 0.83 0.72 0.88 [0.72, 0.88] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²time + σ²residual) 1

They are very similar. Minor differences in the confidence intervals do not impact conclusions.

Consistency ICCs

Generalizability coefficients or Consistency ICCs for situations where absolute agreement is not necessary, as recommended by tenHove 2024.

# Run ICC analysis on all stimuli / dx
icc_C1 <- all_data |>
  group_nest(stimuli, dx) |>
  mutate(
    icc_results = map(data, ~calc_icc_formula_binomial(cbind(score, max_score - score) ~ 1 + time + (1|participant), "participant", .x, R = bootstrap_n))
  )

# Extract ICC results table
icc_table_c <- icc_C1 |>
  mutate(icc_data = map(icc_results, "icc_results")) |>
  select(stimuli, dx, icc_data) |>
  unnest(icc_data)

# Extract variance components table  
variance_table_c <- icc_C1 |>
  mutate(var_data = map(icc_results, "variance_components")) |>
  select(stimuli, dx, var_data) |>
  unnest(var_data)

practice_effects <- icc_C1 |>
     mutate(params = map(icc_results, "parameters")) |>
     select(stimuli, dx, params) |>
     unnest(params) |> filter(Effects == "fixed", Parameter == "timet2")

manuscript_table2 = icc_table_c |> 
    select(Stimuli = stimuli, Group = dx, 'Generalizability' = icc, `95% CI` = `ci_95`) |> 
  flextable() |> 
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>   # Make sure table is left-aligned
  flextable::set_caption(apa_caption("Table X. Generalizability Coefficients by Stimuli and Group"), align_with_table = TRUE,
                         word_stylename = "Normal",
                         fp_p = fp_par(text.align = "left")) |>
  flextable::autofit(add_w = 0, add_h = 1) |>
  flextable::add_footer_lines(values = "Note: Generalizability coefficients are analogous to intraclass correlations for consistency") |>
  theme_apa2()

# To save it as a word document:
doc <- officer::read_docx()
# add the table to the document
doc <- body_add_flextable(doc, value = manuscript_table2)
# filename within the path we want
print(doc, target = "output/icc_by_stimuli_and_group_consistency_revision.docx")


knitr::kable(icc_table_c |> select(-ci_lower, -ci_upper))
stimuli dx icc ci_95 formula bootstrap_success_rate
Cat control 0.65 [0.47, 0.74] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Cat pwa 0.88 [0.81, 0.92] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Cinderella control 0.89 [0.8, 0.93] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Cinderella pwa 0.97 [0.94, 0.98] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Sandwich control 0.60 [0.42, 0.69] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Sandwich pwa 0.85 [0.77, 0.89] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Umbrella control 0.56 [0.4, 0.66] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Umbrella pwa 0.88 [0.78, 0.92] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Window control 0.49 [0.34, 0.58] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
Window pwa 0.83 [0.71, 0.89] σ²participant / (σ²participant + σ²residual) 1
  • here’s a table of both sets of coefficients:
icc_all = icc_table |> 
  select(stimuli, dx, icc_a1 = icc, ci_95_a1 = ci_95) |> 
  left_join(
    icc_table_c |> select(stimuli, dx, icc_c1 = icc, ci_95_c1 = ci_95),
    by = join_by(stimuli, dx)
  )

manuscript_table4 = icc_all |> 
  rename(Stimuli = stimuli, Group = dx) |> 
  flextable() |> 
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  compose(
    j = "icc_a1",
    part = "header",
    #value = as_paragraph("ICC", as_sub("A,1"))
    value = as_paragraph("Dependability")
  ) |>
  compose(
    j = "ci_95_a1", 
    part = "header",
    #value = as_paragraph("95% CI", as_sub("A,1"))
    value = as_paragraph("95% CI")
  ) |>
  compose(
    j = "icc_c1",
    part = "header", 
    #value = as_paragraph("ICC", as_sub("C,1"))
    value = as_paragraph("Generalizability")
  ) |>
  compose(
    j = "ci_95_c1",
    part = "header",
   # value = as_paragraph("95% CI", as_sub("C,1"))
       value = as_paragraph("95% CI")

  ) |>
  flextable::set_caption(apa_caption("Table 3. Estimates of Dependability and Generalizability by stimulus for individuals with aphasia and healthy controls"), 
                         align_with_table = TRUE,
                         word_stylename = "Normal",
                         fp_p = fp_par(text.align = "left")) |>
  flextable::autofit(add_w = 0, add_h = 1) |>
    flextable::add_footer_lines(values = "Note: Dependability coefficients are analogous to single-rater agreement intraclass correlation coefficients and Generalizability coefficients are analogous to single-rater consistency intraclass correlation coefficients. ") |>
  theme_apa2()

# To save it as a word document:
doc <- officer::read_docx()
# add the table to the document
doc <- body_add_flextable(doc, value = manuscript_table4)
# filename within the path we want
print(doc, target = "output/icc_by_stimuli_and_group_all-revision.docx")

Note: Dependability coefficients are analogous to single-rater agreement intraclass correlation coefficients and Generalizability coefficients are analogous to single-rater consistency intraclass correlation coefficients.

We can also get estimates of “time” to examine practice effects directly:

time_table = practice_effects <- icc_C1 |>
     mutate(params = map(icc_results, "parameters")) |>
     select(stimuli, dx, params) |>
     unnest(params) |> filter(Effects == "fixed", Parameter == "timet2") |> 
     format_table()

time_table_manuscript = time_table |> 
  select(-Effects, -df, -z, -Group, Stimuli = stimuli) |> 
  rename(Group = dx) |> 
  flextable() |> 
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>   # Make sure table is left-aligned
  flextable::set_caption(apa_caption("Supplemental Table 1. Fixed effects of time for all stimuli and groups"), align_with_table = TRUE,
                         word_stylename = "Normal",
                         fp_p = fp_par(text.align = "left")) |>
  flextable::autofit(add_w = 0, add_h = 1) |>
  flextable::add_footer_lines(values = "") |>
  theme_apa2()

# To save it as a word document:
doc <- officer::read_docx()
# add the table to the document
doc <- body_add_flextable(doc, value = time_table_manuscript)
# filename within the path we want
print(doc, target = "output/supplement-time-1_revision.docx")

knitr::kable(time_table)
stimuli dx Parameter Coefficient SE 95% CI z df p Effects Group
Cat control timet2 0.11 0.08 [-0.04, 0.27] 1.43 Inf 0.154 fixed
Cat pwa timet2 0.06 0.07 [-0.07, 0.19] 0.84 Inf 0.403 fixed
Cinderella control timet2 0.10 0.05 [ 0.00, 0.21] 1.99 Inf 0.047 fixed
Cinderella pwa timet2 0.12 0.04 [ 0.04, 0.20] 2.87 Inf 0.004 fixed
Sandwich control timet2 -0.12 0.10 [-0.31, 0.07] -1.23 Inf 0.220 fixed
Sandwich pwa timet2 0.07 0.08 [-0.09, 0.23] 0.83 Inf 0.407 fixed
Umbrella control timet2 0.10 0.08 [-0.04, 0.25] 1.37 Inf 0.170 fixed
Umbrella pwa timet2 0.11 0.07 [-0.02, 0.23] 1.59 Inf 0.111 fixed
Window control timet2 0.17 0.10 [-0.03, 0.36] 1.69 Inf 0.092 fixed
Window pwa timet2 -0.04 0.08 [-0.20, 0.12] -0.54 Inf 0.593 fixed

Overall effects including all stimuli

Looking at reliability as if stimuli were facets. This includes estimates with and without group as a fixed effect

# first we have to standardize the scores. I've done this by calculating % 
# accuracy using the max possible scores. this normalizes the scores accounting
# for stimuli length. 

icc_5 = calc_icc_formula_binomial(
  formula = cbind(score, max_score-score) ~ (1|participant) + (1|stimuli) + (1|stimuli:participant) + (1|time) + (1|time:participant) + (1|time:stimuli),
  icc_facet = "participant",
  R = bootstrap_n,
  data = all_data
)

icc_5_dx = calc_icc_formula_binomial(
  formula = cbind(score, max_score-score) ~ 1 + dx + (1|participant) + (1|stimuli)+ (1|stimuli:participant),
  icc_facet = "participant",
  R = bootstrap_n,
  data = all_data
)
knitr::kable(bind_rows(icc_5$icc_results |> mutate(method = "collapsed") |> select(-ci_upper, -ci_lower),
                       icc_5_dx$icc_results |> mutate(method = "dx-removed") |> select(-ci_upper, -ci_lower)
                       ))
icc ci_95 formula bootstrap_success_rate method
0.76 [0.67, 0.82] σ²participant / (σ²stimuli:participant + σ²time:participant + σ²participant + σ²time:stimuli + σ²stimuli + σ²time + σ²residual) 1 collapsed
0.71 [0.6, 0.78] σ²participant / (σ²stimuli:participant + σ²participant + σ²stimuli + σ²residual) 1 dx-removed

Then we split out pwa and control particpants:

pwa = all_data |> filter(dx == "pwa")
control = all_data |> filter(dx == "control")

# Call function
icc_5_pwa <- calc_icc_formula_binomial(
  formula = cbind(score, max_score-score) ~ (1|participant) + (1|stimuli) + (1|stimuli:participant) + (1|time) + (1|time:participant) + (1|time:stimuli),
  icc_facet = "participant",
  R = bootstrap_n,
  data = pwa
)

# Call function
icc_5_control <- calc_icc_formula_binomial(
  formula = cbind(score, max_score-score) ~ (1|participant) + (1|stimuli) + (1|stimuli:participant) + (1|time) + (1|time:participant) + (1|time:stimuli),
  icc_facet = "participant",
  R = bootstrap_n,
  data = control
)

Decision study

  • what would be the effects of averaging 1…5 stimuli on reliability?
  • we can use the models and bootstrapped iterations to derive these estimates and uncertainty values
# Get decision study results instantly (no refitting!)
decision_study_pwa <- calc_decision_study_from_bootstrap(icc_5_pwa) |> mutate(group = "pwa")
decision_study_pwa 
# A tibble: 5 × 6
  n_stimuli phi_coefficient ci_lower ci_upper ci_95        group
      <int>           <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>        <chr>
1         1            0.89     0.82     0.92 [0.82, 0.92] pwa  
2         2            0.94     0.89     0.96 [0.89, 0.96] pwa  
3         3            0.95     0.92     0.97 [0.92, 0.97] pwa  
4         4            0.96     0.93     0.98 [0.93, 0.98] pwa  
5         5            0.97     0.94     0.98 [0.94, 0.98] pwa  
# Get decision study results instantly (no refitting!)
decision_study_control <- calc_decision_study_from_bootstrap(icc_5_control) |> mutate(group = "control")
decision_study_control
# A tibble: 5 × 6
  n_stimuli phi_coefficient ci_lower ci_upper ci_95        group  
      <int>           <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>        <chr>  
1         1            0.51     0.36     0.62 [0.36, 0.62] control
2         2            0.67     0.52     0.76 [0.52, 0.76] control
3         3            0.74     0.6      0.82 [0.6, 0.82]  control
4         4            0.78     0.65     0.86 [0.65, 0.86] control
5         5            0.81     0.68     0.88 [0.68, 0.88] control
ds = bind_rows(
  decision_study_pwa,
  decision_study_control
)

knitr::kable(ds)
n_stimuli phi_coefficient ci_lower ci_upper ci_95 group
1 0.89 0.82 0.92 [0.82, 0.92] pwa
2 0.94 0.89 0.96 [0.89, 0.96] pwa
3 0.95 0.92 0.97 [0.92, 0.97] pwa
4 0.96 0.93 0.98 [0.93, 0.98] pwa
5 0.97 0.94 0.98 [0.94, 0.98] pwa
1 0.51 0.36 0.62 [0.36, 0.62] control
2 0.67 0.52 0.76 [0.52, 0.76] control
3 0.74 0.60 0.82 [0.6, 0.82] control
4 0.78 0.65 0.86 [0.65, 0.86] control
5 0.81 0.68 0.88 [0.68, 0.88] control
  • and plot the results:
ds_plot = ds |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = n_stimuli, y = phi_coefficient, color = group)) +
  geom_point( position = position_dodge(0.3)) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 0.3, position = position_dodge(0.3)) + 
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1), limits = c(0,1)) + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") + 
  labs(y = "Estimate of Reliability", x = "Number of Stimuli")

ggsave(plot = ds_plot, filename = here::here("output", "decision-study-revision.png"))
Saving 7 x 5 in image
ds_plot

MDC

Calculating MDC for each stimuli. MDC 95 can also be derived from the initial models; calcuating the SEM

  • this returns estimates of MDC in the number of items for 5-item ranges throughout the range of possible options
mdc <- all_data |>
  group_by(stimuli, dx) |>
  summarise(
    mdc_logit = calc_mdc_formula_binomial(
      formula = cbind(score, max_score - score) ~ (1|participant) + (1|time),
      icc_facet = "participant",
      exclude_from_error = NULL,
      data = cur_data()
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  left_join(all_data |> distinct(stimuli, max_score), by = "stimuli")

# Step 2: Create MDC lookup tables by baseline
mdc_tables <- calc_mdc_by_baseline(mdc)
readr::write_csv(mdc_tables, here::here("output", "Supplement 2 - MDC by stimuli.csv"))
  
# Create plot data
plot_data <- mdc |>
  mutate(
    plot_data = map(mdc_logit, function(mdc_log) {
      tibble(baseline = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.01)) |>
        mutate(
          baseline_logit = qlogis(baseline),
          lower_pct = plogis(baseline_logit - mdc_log),
          upper_pct = plogis(baseline_logit + mdc_log)
        )
    })
  ) |>
  select(stimuli, dx, plot_data) |>
  unnest(plot_data)

head(mdc_tables)
# A tibble: 6 × 7
  stimuli dx      max_score stratum score_range `mdc (items)` mdc_percentage
  <fct>   <fct>       <dbl>   <int> <chr>               <dbl>          <dbl>
1 Cat     control        35       1 0-5                     5          0.139
2 Cat     control        35       2 6-10                    7          0.200
3 Cat     control        35       3 11-15                   8          0.210
4 Cat     control        35       4 16-20                   8          0.204
5 Cat     control        35       5 21-25                   7          0.179
6 Cat     control        35       6 26-30                   5          0.129
# Create plot
p = ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = baseline)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower_pct, ymax = upper_pct, fill = dx), 
              alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = baseline), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_line(aes(y = lower_pct, color = dx)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = upper_pct, color = dx)) +
  facet_grid(dx~stimuli) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.spacing.x = unit(1.5, "lines")) +
  labs(
    x = "Baseline Score (Time 1)",
    y = "Score Range Including MDC",
    title = "Minimal Detectable Change (95% CI) by Baseline Score",
    fill = "Group",
    color = "Group"
  )

ggsave(plot = p, filename = "MDC-by-stimuli-baseline.png")
Saving 7 x 5 in image
p
p = all_wide |> 
  mutate(dx = ifelse(dx == "control", "Healthy Control", "Individuals with Aphasia")) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = t1, y = t2, fill = dx)) +
  #geom_point() + 
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.8, size = 2, shape = 21, color = "lightgrey", width = .015, height = 0.015) +
  geom_abline(alpha = 0.6) + 
  geom_ribbon(inherit.aes = FALSE,
              data = plot_data |> rename(t1 = baseline) |> mutate(dx = ifelse(dx == "control", "Healthy Control", "Individuals with Aphasia")),
              aes(x = t1, ymin = lower_pct, ymax = upper_pct, fill = dx),
              alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_grid(dx~stimuli) + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), labels = scales::label_percent(), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), labels = scales::label_percent(), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", aspect.ratio = 1) +
  labs(fill = "", x = "Percent of Checklist Produced, Time 1", y = "Percent of Checklist Produced, Time 2") +
  coord_fixed()

ggsave(plot = p, filename = here::here("output", "figure 1 - revision.png"), width = 12, height = 10)

p
sems = all_data |>
  group_by(stimuli, dx) |>
  summarise(
    sem_logit = calc_sem(
      formula = cbind(score, max_score - score) ~ (1|participant) + (1|time),
      icc_facet = "participant",
      exclude_from_error = NULL,
      data = cur_data()
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(sems)
stimuli dx sem_logit
Cat control 0.3092480
Cat pwa 0.3065880
Cinderella control 0.1945994
Cinderella pwa 0.2001493
Sandwich control 0.3636117
Sandwich pwa 0.3627599
Umbrella control 0.3040321
Umbrella pwa 0.3065715
Window control 0.3757798
Window pwa 0.3702402 
mdc_all <- mdc_tables |> rename(mdc = `mdc (items)`)
save.image(here::here("output", "output-2-5.RData"))
